steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage asks which species is "least suited to a full migration to the mesophotic zone," defined as 30–150 meters deep. A species currently confined entirely above 30 meters has never experienced mesophotic conditions, making it the worst candidate for that migration. The table's depth ranges determine the answer.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Agaricia grahamae, because its minimum depth of 20 meters is in the shallow zone.: Agaricia grahamae's minimum depth is 20 m (shallow), but its maximum depth extends into the mesophotic zone — meaning it already lives there. It is not the least suited. ✗
  • Choice B — Acropora anthocercis, because its maximum depth of 10 meters is furthest from the mesophotic zone.: Acropora anthocercis tops out at just 10 meters — entirely within the shallow zone, with no overlap with the mesophotic zone at all. It has had zero exposure to mesophotic conditions, making it the least suited for full migration. ✓
  • Choice C — Acropora striata, because its maximum depth of 25 meters is close to but doesn't reach the mesophotic zone.: Acropora striata reaches 25 meters, closer to the 30-meter threshold than Acropora anthocercis's 10-meter maximum. It is better suited for migration than Choice B — Acropora anthocercis, because its maximum depth of 10 meters is furthest from the mesophotic zone.'s species. ✗
  • Choice D — Danafungia scruposa, because its minimum depth of 1 meter is the shallowest of those listed.: A shallow minimum depth doesn't determine suitability — what matters is whether the species reaches mesophotic depths at all. The minimum depth alone is irrelevant to the migration question. ✗
That matches Choice B — Acropora anthocercis, because its maximum depth of 10 meters is furthest from the mesophotic zone..
